meta.title: 225xe/225e Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: BMW 225xe/225e PHEV — real UK costs, battery & timing chain insight, and honest reliability data for the B38 hybrid powertrain. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 225xe/225e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B38 engine typically costs £2,500–£4,500 supply only. A complete PHEV powertrain (engine + electric motor) costs £4,000–£7,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on your location and workshop. The high-voltage battery is a separate component — replacement typically costs £4,000–£8,000.

The B38 three-cylinder petrol engine is generally reliable — it's a proven, modern BMW engine with no major systemic failures. However, the hybrid system introduces complexity: battery degradation, timing cover oil leaks, and occasional electric motor faults are the main concerns. The petrol engine itself is the more robust element of the package.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 225xe/225e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are high-voltage battery degradation (reduced electric range), timing cover oil leaks on the B38 engine, timing chain tensioner wear, and occasional electric motor or inverter faults. The battery is the single biggest long-term concern — it can cost more to replace than the car is worth on older examples.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 225xe/225e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on which component has failed. Against a well-kept £18,000 225xe, a £4,000 B38 replacement is 22% — worth it. But if the battery has failed (£4,000–£8,000), the maths changes dramatically. A £7,000 battery replacement against a £12,000 car is 58% — approaching our 70% BMW threshold for PHEVs. Get a battery health report before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 225xe/225e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. F45 generation (2016–2021): B38A15A. U06 generation (2022–present): B38A15P. The chassis code is the easiest tell — F45 is the first-generation Active Tourer, U06 is the second-generation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing cover oil leak, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — timing cover gasket replacement is a well-understood repair costing £500–£1,200. It's the B38's most common oil leak. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have it inspected promptly. It's a straightforward fix, not an engine killer.

rows.eraNote: Early PHEV — 220 hp system output. Battery degradation and timing cover leaks known issues.
Revised PHEV — 245 hp system output. Updated B38 with 7-speed DCT.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

rows.bestFor: B38 engine failure — most common scenario

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the B38 engine and PHEV powertrain. The high-voltage battery is a separate component — replacement costs typically range from £4,000–£8,000 depending on battery condition and supplier.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 225xe/225e powertrain typically takes 14–20 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). High-voltage system work requires specialist training and equipment.

cta.label: → Compare 225xe/225e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: High-Voltage Battery Degradation (F45 & U06)
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric-only range, rapid battery depletion under load, drivetrain error messages, inability to hold charge.
problems.recommendation: Battery degradation is the single biggest concern on the 225xe/225e. The usable capacity of the high-voltage battery is approximately 5.6 kWh when new, and BMW guarantees 70% capacity after 6 years or 100,000 km. If your electric range has dropped significantly, have the battery inspected. Extended warranties typically do not cover battery issues.

failureLink.label: Read the full battery degradation guide

problems.title: Timing Cover Oil Leak — B38 Engine
problems.symptoms: Oil drips on the garage floor, burning oil smell after driving, oil traces around the timing cover.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,200 (timing cover gasket replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The B38's timing cover gasket is known to fail, allowing oil to leak. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the timing cover inspected. Left unchecked, oil loss can lead to low oil levels and potential engine damage — though this doesn't typically progress to full engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: Electric Motor / High-Voltage System Faults
problems.symptoms: Drivetrain warning light, loss of electric power, unusual whining or grinding from the rear axle, reduced performance.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (electric motor or inverter replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear unusual noises or lose power, stop and book inspection

problems.recommendation: Electric motor and inverter faults are rare but can occur. Symptoms include drivetrain errors and unusual noises from the rear of the vehicle. If you experience these, have the vehicle inspected immediately — high-voltage system faults can be dangerous and expensive.

failureLink.label: Read the full electric motor failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 225xe/225e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with evidence of regular hybrid system checks
* No drivetrain warning lights or error codes
* Evidence of regular oil changes using BMW LL-01 approved oil
avoidIf: * Drivetrain warning light with no diagnosis — could indicate expensive hybrid system faults
* Significantly reduced electric range (battery degradation warning)
* No service history — the B38 is reliable but the hybrid system demands proper maintenance
* Unusual whining or grinding from the rear axle (electric motor warning)

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 225xe/225e is BMW's compact PHEV MPV — a clever blend of the reliable B38 three-cylinder petrol engine and an electric rear-axle motor. The petrol engine itself is robust, but the hybrid system introduces complexity. Battery degradation is the single biggest long-term concern — the battery is worth more than the car on older examples. Buy on documented battery health, not on faith. A well-maintained 225xe/225e is a £15,000–£25,000 family car; a neglected one with a dying battery is a £5,000 gamble.

cta.label: → Compare 225xe/225e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 225xe/225e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — Ask for a battery health report; 70%+ capacity after 6 years is BMW's warranty standard
* Electric Range — Test the electric-only range; significant reduction indicates battery degradation
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling from the B38 engine (timing chain tensioner wear warning)
* Service History — including the PHEV era and confirmed oil-change intervals using BMW LL-01 approved oil
* Drivetrain Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved hybrid system issues?
* Rear Axle Noise — Any whining or grinding from the rear? (electric motor warning)
* Oil Leak Check — Any evidence of oil around the timing cover?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
